opp.ais_why:
Strong platform momentum with CFO sponsorship—'The CFO has had a conversation... to say that this is a priority'—and engagement with GenAI/autonomous negotiation agents during demos.
AI-trust friction tempers excitement: 'I'd imagine they'd be hugely wary about an agent, an AI agent marking someone's tender'; adoption framed as gradual and human-in-loop.
AI interest is real but secondary to P2P/compliance (Procurement Act, CDP). Three transcribed calls give high confidence.

items.quote: The CFO has had a conversation with me and my counterpart to say that this is a priority in terms of the system.
I can't give you a finite date on terms of when it's gonna happen.
we're coming towards the end of the discovery phase In terms of all the providers that that that we've seen.

items.area: Only 2 buyer-side contact roles on the opportunity, below multi-thread bar

items.quote: If you want a business case, we can actually do a value business case so that you are actually doing a ROI together.

metrics.narrative: The buyer has articulated qualitative pain clearly but has not quantified the value case. On 09 Mar 2026 Darren Stanley framed the ROI logic himself: 'if it starts to pay for itself and you come back to your ROI and the payback is, you know, you're talking months as opposed to years, then it's a no brainer for an organization.' Zycus asked for total supplier spend (including February figures) to build a value business case and ROI model, and Darren committed to send it, but no quantified savings, cycle-time, or headcount numbers are on record. The next move is to convert the requested spend data into a quantified ROI model the senior leadership business case can carry.

champion.narrative: Darren Stanley, the procurement consultant brought in to lead this stream, is the active champion: he set up the calls, drove the agenda, pushed internally for the business case, and has kept the dialogue open through the slowdown ('the fact that we're still chatting is a positive', 11 Jun 2026). However his advocacy is constrained, he is a contractor 'further removed from what's actually happening' with no power over EMT prioritization, and engagement is single-threaded to him. The March evaluators Konrad Adamski and Anastasia Jensen showed strong interest but have not appeared since 24 Mar 2026 (~3 months). The gap is a powered internal champion with CFO/EMT access; building one is a ranked move.

competition.narrative: No competitor has been named in any call, field, task, or next step, so the competitive field is genuinely unknown rather than empty by omission. The buyer confirmed it is evaluating multiple providers in discovery (Darren Stanley, 11 Jun 2026: 'all the providers that we've seen') and that a formal tender will run, which will surface the full shortlist when issued. Zycus has no confirmation of preferred or sole-source status. For now this is a multi-vendor discovery with rivals undisclosed; the move is to map the shortlist before the RFP locks criteria, not to assert a competitor the evidence does not name.

sources: Avoma sourcing & CLM session 24 Mar 2026 (Darren Stanley)
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well articulated. The current requisition process is email-driven via Outlook leading to extensive back-and-forth with managers (06 Mar 2026). Tail spend is a stated challenge: small-dollar purchases that consume disproportionate time and resource (Darren Stanley, 24 Mar 2026). On contract management, the buyer described struggles with their contract repository needing clarity on terms, costing time and money (24 Mar 2026). Underlying all of it is a post-regulatory-scrutiny housing association under reputational and operational pressure across 38,000 homes (Darren Stanley, 11 Jun 2026). Pain is real but currently outranked by competing change programs, which is why urgency is muted.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are partially mapped. A2Dominion is in a regulated space and will run a formal tender under the Procurement Act because three-year-lifecycle spend crosses statutory thresholds (Darren Stanley, 11 Jun 2026). Implementation will involve A2Dominion's internal IT function within the project team, with Zycus offering either direct SaaS delivery or a GSI-partnered model (09 Mar 2026). An NDA was referenced in the Next Step in connection with a possible paid proof-of-concept. No MSA/SOW, legal redline, or InfoSec process has started because the deal has not reached a buying decision. Visibility would improve by confirming the tender timeline and the procurement owner's process in writing.

economic_buyer.narrative: The Economic Buyer is the A2Dominion CFO, named on 09 Mar 2026 by Darren Stanley: 'The CFO has had a conversation with me and my counterpart to say that this is a priority in terms of the system.' The CFO's specific name was not disclosed and the CFO has never appeared on a call. Budget authority sits with the senior leadership / EMT, who are continuously reprioritizing the change portfolio (Darren Stanley, 11 Jun 2026). So the EB role is identified but unengaged: there is no direct pricing, budget, or ROI conversation with the CFO on record. Getting a named CFO contact and a direct value conversation is the highest-leverage power move.

decision_process.narrative: The process is reconstructable from the call sequence and is explicitly pre-procurement. A2Dominion is finishing a discovery phase across multiple providers (Darren Stanley, 11 Jun 2026: 'we're coming towards the end of the discovery phase in terms of all the providers that we've seen'). The next deliverable is an outline business case / options paper for senior (EMT) review, after which an RFP/RFQ may follow. Because the spend over a three-year lifecycle exceeds Procurement Act thresholds, a formal tender is required and final ownership returns to procurement (Claire Slater referenced as the contracting owner). The blocker is that procurement is gated behind organizational stabilization following prior regulatory scrutiny, with no firm RFP date.

sources: Avoma sourcing & CLM session 24 Mar 2026 (Konrad Adamski, Gabriela, Anastasia Jensen)
decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are well evidenced across the demo and sourcing/CLM sessions. Primary scope is P2P (eProcurement plus invoicing with three-way matching), with contract management and sourcing as exploratory add-ons (Nishita Sareen / Darren Stanley, 09 Mar 2026). On 24 Mar 2026 Konrad Adamski stressed AI trust as a criterion: the need for consistent scoring, redlining guidance and clear policy enforcement to gain evaluator confidence, and Gabriela emphasised strict Procurement Act compliance with integration to the UK Central Digital Platform (CDP) for tender-notice publication. Anastasia Jensen championed quality over quantity, wanting AI tools that free the team for strategic work and reduce manual error. Microsoft Dynamics F&O integration was confirmed and a Total Mobile work-order connection was raised (06 Mar 2026).

deal_movement.summary: Strong early momentum (three substantive sessions in March) has cooled into a holding pattern: the buyer paused procurement behind internal change, and recent touches are check-ins rather than advancing milestones. Engagement remains live (touchpoints on 11 Jun and 30 Jun 2026) but no buying step has progressed.

items.detail: Decision timeline is indefinite; deal stalled pending EMT prioritization of competing change programs with no firm RFP issue date. Consultant confirmed on 11 Jun 'I can't give you a finite date on when it's gonna happen' and noted senior leadership continuously reprioritizes the change portfolio based on operational pain and reputational risk. Prior regulatory scrutiny and leadership changes are delaying system-replacement approval. Close date Mar 2027 is a placeholder; realistic timeline cannot be assessed.

CFO (Economic Buyer) has never appeared on a call; no pricing, budget, or ROI discussion with EB on record. Consultant is facilitating discovery but has no decision power and is 'further removed from what's actually happening.' Without EB engagement and approval, deal cannot move to the relevant stakeholder or Negotiation.

System replacement is one of many competing change programs in a post-regulatory-scrutiny environment; organizational stabilization and other strategic changes must happen first. Senior leadership (EMT) is continuously reprioritizing the change portfolio, and the S2P project is not yet approved. Consultant confirmed 'your top 10 isn't your top 10 probably at the end of that week' because operational demands (38,000 housing units, resident services, reputational risk) reorder priorities daily.

Single-threaded to consultant with no champion who has EB/DM access or power to influence EMT prioritization. Konrad Adamski and Anastasia Jensen (evaluators) engaged in March but silent for 3 months. Only 2 buyer-side contact roles on opp, well below multi-thread bar for £150k enterprise deal. No internal advocate to push the deal when change portfolio is reprioritized.

Budget envelope £200k–£1.5M SaaS stated by consultant (lower end expected), but no CFO sign-off on that range, no formal budget allocation confirmed, and no ROI quantification to anchor the investment. Business-case options paper in progress but not yet reviewed by senior leadership.

Competitive field unknown (no competitor named); formal RFP/tender will disclose full vendor shortlist and could introduce stronger rivals when issued. Zycus has no confirmation of preferred-vendor status or sole-source consideration.
No firm RFP/procurement date; procurement deferred behind organizational stabilization and competing change programs.
CFO (Economic Buyer) named but never engaged directly; no budget sign-off or ROI conversation with EB.
Single-threaded to a contractor with no EMT power; evaluators silent since March.
Budget envelope £200k-£1.5M stated by consultant (lower end expected) but no formal allocation or CFO sign-off and no ROI anchor.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live and friendly but momentum has cooled into holding-pattern check-ins; no buying-stage progression since March. Cadence slowed from three substantive sessions in March to catch-ups in June.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 12 Mar 2026, in Formal Evaluation ~3.5 months (since 12 Mar). Close date 12 Mar 2027 is 255 days out and is a placeholder. Last buyer touch was a scheduled check-in on 30 Jun 2026 (today); last substantive buyer call was 11 Jun 2026 (~19 days ago) where the consultant confirmed no firm RFP date. Six buyer touchpoints discovered, four read. Pace is not consistent with a 12-month enterprise motion reaching close by Mar 2027 because the buyer has explicitly deferred procurement behind other change programs.
evidence: Darren Stanley, 11 Jun 2026: 'we're not at a point right here, now to press a button on a RFP, RFQ' and 'I can't give you a finite date on terms of when it's gonna happen.'
Darren Stanley, 11 Jun 2026: 'the system that we're looking to replace is just one of a whole raft of change processes that are on the change portfolio, and they're all fighting for airtime.'
Darren Stanley, 09 Mar 2026: 'The CFO has had a conversation with me and my counterpart to say that this is a priority in terms of the system.'
north_star_verdict.headline: Live discovery with strong engagement, but buyer has paused procurement pending internal change; no RFP date and no EB on a call.

flags: Map and engage the CFO (Economic Buyer) directly with an ROI workshop or executive briefing before the RFP is issued. The CFO sponsored the project (confirmed by consultant 9 Mar) but has never appeared on a call, and no commercial or budget discussion has occurred with them. Without EB engagement on pricing, ROI, and timing, the deal cannot move to the relevant stakeholder or Negotiation, and Zycus has no line of sight to whether the CFO will approve the £150k–£1.5M investment once the change-portfolio prioritization is resolved. Activation: when Darren confirms the business-case options paper has been reviewed by senior leadership and the project is re-prioritized, immediately request a CFO touchpoint (co-facilitated with Darren) to anchor the commercial conversation and validate budget authority.
Single-threaded to a contractor with no EMT power: secure a direct line to the CFO (named EB) or an internal A2Dominion owner before the change portfolio is reprioritized again.
best_practice_check.summary: Top win-strategy levers as of 30 Jun 2026: break out of single-threading to the consultant by getting CFO/EMT access, convert engagement into a value-led business case to win prioritization, and shape the RFP criteria before it issues.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor has been named in any source (Salesforce field empty, no call mention). Darren Stanley confirmed on 11 Jun 2026 that A2Dominion is evaluating multiple providers in discovery phase, meaning Zycus is in a competitive pool, but the identity, count, and threat level of rivals are unknown. The only competitive signal is that a formal RFP/tender will be required (per UK Procurement Act thresholds) once EMT approves the change program, at which point the full vendor shortlist will be disclosed. Current positioning: Zycus demonstrated strong fit on AI/Merlin capabilities (genAI requisition, AI contract analysis, compliance automation with CDP API integration), and buyer engagement was highest on those topics (24 Mar call), but no confirmation of preferred-vendor status or sole-source consideration. Competitive threat level: unknown. The lack of competitor intelligence is a genuine knowledge gap, not a hygiene issue, because Darren has not disclosed the field and no other buyer speaker has mentioned a rival.

Strong fit; the gap is converting interest into a quantified value case.

AI interest is real but secondary to P2P/compliance (Procurement Act, CDP). Three transcribed calls give high confidence.

deltas.to: Darren Stanley - developing
deltas.subject: champion

Multi-thread aggressively beyond the consultant to build advocacy with power roles who can influence EMT prioritization. Darren Stanley (consultant) is the sole active thread and has explicitly stated he is 'further removed from what's actually happening' and has no decision power. Konrad Adamski (Procurement Manager) and Anastasia Jensen (Interim Procurement Analyst) engaged deeply in the March demos but have been silent for 3 months (last contact 24 Mar). Only 2 buyer-side contact roles are logged on this opp, well below the multi-thread bar for a £150k enterprise deal with a 12–15 month cycle. Without a champion who has EB/DM access and can advocate for Zycus inside the EMT change-portfolio debate, the deal will remain in holding pattern or lose to a more aggressive competitor when the RFP is issued. Activation: request Darren to facilitate introductions to the CFO, the head of procurement operations (Konrad's manager or peer), and a senior finance/operations leader who sits on the EMT and can champion the system-replacement business case internally.

Consultant confirmed 'your top 10 isn't your top 10 probably at the end of that week' because operational demands (38,000 housing units, resident services, reputational risk) reorder priorities daily.

deltas.to: Map and engage the CFO (Economic Buyer) directly with an ROI workshop or executive briefing before the RFP is issued. The CFO sponsored the project (confirmed b
deltas.subject: Map and engage the CFO (Economic Buyer) directly with an ROI workshop or executive briefing before the RFP is issued. The CFO sponsored the project (confirmed by consultant 9 Mar) but has never appeared on a call, and no commercial or budget discussion has occurred with them. Without EB engagement on pricing, ROI, and timing, the deal cannot move to the relevant stakeholder or Negotiation, and Zycus has no line of sight to whether the CFO will approve the £150k–£1.5M investment once the change-portfolio prioritization is resolved. Activation: when Darren confirms the business-case options paper has been reviewed by senior leadership and the project is re-prioritized, immediately request a CFO touchpoint (co-facilitated with Darren) to anchor the commercial conversation and validate budget authority.

deltas.to: Multi-thread aggressively beyond the consultant to build advocacy with power roles who can influence EMT prioritization. Darren Stanley (consultant) is the sole
deltas.subject: Multi-thread aggressively beyond the consultant to build advocacy with power roles who can influence EMT prioritization. Darren Stanley (consultant) is the sole active thread and has explicitly stated he is 'further removed from what's actually happening' and has no decision power. Konrad Adamski (Procurement Manager) and Anastasia Jensen (Interim Procurement Analyst) engaged deeply in the March demos but have been silent for 3 months (last contact 24 Mar). Only 2 buyer-side contact roles are logged on this opp, well below the multi-thread bar for a £150k enterprise deal with a 12–15 month cycle. Without a champion who has EB/DM access and can advocate for Zycus inside the EMT change-portfolio debate, the deal will remain in holding pattern or lose to a more aggressive competitor when the RFP is issued. Activation: request Darren to facilitate introductions to the CFO, the head of procurement operations (Konrad's manager or peer), and a senior finance/operations leader who sits on the EMT and can champion the system-replacement business case internally.

deltas.to: Differentiate on AI/Merlin and compliance automation (Zycus's strongest positioning from the demos) by making those capabilities the centerpiece of the business
deltas.subject: Differentiate on AI/Merlin and compliance automation (Zycus's strongest positioning from the demos) by making those capabilities the centerpiece of the business-case narrative and the paid POC (if executed). Buyer engagement was highest on genAI requisition (6 Mar), AI contract clause analysis and trust-building (24 Mar, Konrad Adamski and Anastasia Jensen), and UK Procurement Act compliance automation with the relevant stakeholder integration (24 Mar, Gabriela flagged as critical). When the RFP is issued, the competitive field will be disclosed, and Zycus will need a clear wedge. The AI/Merlin story (autonomous negotiation agent for tail spend, AI-driven questionnaire builder, Merlin Risk Radar, AI contract redlining with trust-building guidance) and the compliance-automation story (policy knowledge base, CDP API for public tender publishing, Cyber Essentials/G-Cloud pursuit for public sector) are Zycus's differentiation vs. traditional P2P players. Activation: when the paid POC is proposed (Next_Step 25 Jun: 'Pulling together paid POC; run it past consultant to see if that forces them to move faster'), design it as a 2-week AI/Merlin showcase (genAI requisition + AI contract analysis + tail-spend negotiation agent) with daily buyer participation (not a passive trial) to create undeniable proof of ROI and build champion conviction before the RFP competition begins.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain (email-driven requisitions, tail spend, contract repository clarity) and a self-stated payback-in-months logic articulated, but no quantified ROI; spend data for a value case still outstanding (09 Mar 2026).

gaps: CFO economic buyer name not disclosed on any call.
Two touchpoints (25 Mar check-in not_recorded; 30 Jun check-in scheduled) have no content.
No competitor named in any source; competitive shortlist undisclosed until tender issues.

customer_preference.evidence: Darren Stanley praised the demo professionalism and platform flexibility ('Ashish, you did really well... demonstrate the real flexibility of the Zycus system') on 09 Mar 2026, and engagement remains warm, but no decisive lean vs other providers and the field is still in discovery.
